para_trans: GOVERNMENT ORDER NUMBER: TRANSFER-2326 / P.No.30 / NO.15
The relevant officers mentioned in the table should be immediately deputed to the place of transfer and report to the government by e-mail at napu15.mhpds@gov.in.
3. As the officers concerned mentioned in the said table are periodically transferred, they are transferred to the place of posting.
Maharashtra Civil Service (Retirement Period, Post-Service and Suspension, Dismissal and Retirement from Service)
the period of assumption of office and transfer as provided in the Provisions for the Period of Removal) Rules, 1981.
Subjective travel allowance and other ancillary benefits are admissible.
4. Cancellation of transfer or direct transfer of the transferred officers concerned mentioned in the said table.
Or by attempting to cancel the transfer under indirect pressure, this action is the Maharashtra Civil Service (Conduct).
It is against them as misconduct as it violates Rule 23 of the Rules, 1979.
Those concerned should note that disciplinary action will be taken.
5. Any kind of officers appointed as aforesaid without prior permission.
Leave shall not be granted and shall be granted by the parent office in accordance with Rule 30 (c) of the Maharashtra Civil Services (Leave) Rules, 1981.
No leave applications of posted officers shall be entertained. If such an application is received, it shall be
The registration invoices must be returned by post to the original address concerned.
6. Regulation of transfers of Maharashtra Government employees and in the discharge of government duties.
Provided by the competent authority under the provisions of section 4 and section 6 of the Prevention of Delay Act, 2005
These changes have been made in exercise of the powers conferred. non-compliance with this order.
Serious and non-compliant officer, Departmental Inquiry Rules, 1991.
shall be liable for suspension proceedings under section 2. 1 (2) (v) of section 2.
7. The officers concerned shall join the place of transfer within the prescribed period of assumption of office. Otherwise,
The period of absence shall be deemed to be a non-working day.
The Government Order is available on the website of the Government of Maharashtra, www.maharashtra.gov.in.
